subject: Scheduler/Dispatcher — the event→advisor orchestration layer that makes the wheel self-roll: event taxonomy + routing, latency/idempotency/backoff, cost circuit-breaker, failure recovery, hub-down behavior
title: Scheduler / Dispatcher (the self-rolling coupling)
situation: A blind review (S46, two independent reviewers) named this the SINGLE biggest build risk: v4's autonomy claim rests on event-driven dispatch ('event → wake the right advisor'), but the dispatcher was declared 'NEW' and never architected. Worse, it sat at build step 9 while every advisor (steps 3-10) structurally needs it to run — a hidden dependency cycle. Without it the harness has observability (events) and verification (gates) but no orchestration: you can watch the wheel fail and verify the failure but cannot make it roll on its own. The current solution confirmed the gap (audit S46: 'no centralized scheduler; manual Expediter/Autopilot invocation; relies on outer cron').
why: The dispatcher IS the coupling that turns loosely-connected loops into a self-rolling wheel. It also owns the two safety properties the review flagged as missing: a COST CIRCUIT-BREAKER (a broken scout firing every 5s must not drain the budget) and IDEMPOTENCY/BACKOFF (one mutation must not thrash a full re-analysis). It must be built EARLY (phase 1) and have its own verification gate — the orchestrator that can't be observed or bounded is the most dangerous component.
audience: Ozi — the dispatcher wakes advisors on Ozi's behalf; Ozi remains CoS
every advisor — declares the events it subscribes to + its analysis_trigger; the dispatcher routes to it
Autopilot — invoked by the dispatcher under budget governance
Observability spine — the dispatcher emits typed events for every dispatch decision (no silent orchestration)
User — the circuit-breaker + budget surface on the Attention panel

event_taxonomy_and_routing.principle: A declarative event→advisor routing table (data, not hardcoded). Each advisor registers {subscribes_to:[event types], analysis_trigger, debounce_window, max_concurrency}. The dispatcher matches events to subscribers and wakes them within an SLA.
event_taxonomy_and_routing.dispatch_latency_sla: p95 < 60s from event to advisor wake for priority>=high; lower priorities batched on cadence

idempotency_and_thrash_control.debounce: N identical events within debounce_window collapse to one wake (e.g. 20 lug_mutations in 30s → one Expediter run, not 20)
idempotency_and_thrash_control.idempotency_key: event correlation + advisor; a wake already in-flight for the same key is not re-issued
idempotency_and_thrash_control.data_sufficiency_respect: the dispatcher honors each advisor's analysis_trigger floor — it does not wake an advisor below its minimum-signal threshold (no over-cycling)
idempotency_and_thrash_control.max_concurrency: per-advisor + global cap on simultaneous wakes; excess queued

cost_circuit_breaker.principle: Autonomy is budget-bounded with HARD breakers — the wheel must never roll into cost damage. (Review HIGH: 'a broken scout firing every 5s drains the budget in hours.')
rules: rate cap: max wakes/hour per advisor + global; breach → pause that advisor + Attention alert
burn cap: if projected spend (provider_usage) exceeds the session/day budget threshold, the dispatcher PAUSES autonomous dispatch and raises an Attention item (alert at 80%, pause at 100%)
loop guard: an advisor that wakes->fails->re-emits the triggering event >K times in a window is circuit-broken (paused, escalated) — prevents self-perpetuating loops
all breaker trips emit typed events + land on the Attention surface with the reason + the projected/actual burn

failure_recovery.advisor_liveness: heartbeat per running advisor; silence > 5m → presumed dead → dispatcher requeues its in-flight work + raises Attention
failure_recovery.dispatcher_self_gate: the dispatcher emits a dispatch-audit event per decision (event→matched advisors→wake/skip+reason); a Pattern Gate certifies the dispatcher's own health (it is not exempt from verification)
failure_recovery.replay: dispatch decisions are durable (event bus); after a crash the dispatcher replays unacked wakes from the journal (ties to event-bus durability, observability spec)
failure_recovery.stuck_workflow: a workflow with no step progress in its SLA is flagged halted → Attention
failure_recovery.gate_retry_orchestration: external review (read-only-gate contradiction): the Pattern Gate is Read+Bash and only EMITS halted/escalate events — it does NOT perform retry writes. The dispatcher CONSUMES those events and orchestrates the retry/remediation through a distinct WRITE-AUTHORIZED actor (re-dispatch the step to an executor), honoring the 2-cycle cap. Verify/observe and act/mutate are cleanly separated actors.

hub_down_behavior.principle: The spoke wheel keeps rolling locally when the hub is unreachable (review MED: 'hub failure must not halt the fleet').
rules: spoke-local dispatch + Autopilot + gates continue from the local copy + local SQLite
work needing the hub (fleet evolution canonicalization, cross-spoke delivery) queues with a hub_pending flag
the spoke self-certifies locally with a 'hub-unreachable' marker on its certification; reconciles on hub recovery
hub-down state is itself an observable surface (not silent)

guidance_boundary.autonomous_column: dispatcher freely drains Expediter's 'autonomous' ready-queue under the breakers
guidance_boundary.attention_column: needs-you / sign-off / escalation items are async-batched to the Attention surface, NEVER block the autonomous column
guidance_boundary.response_sla: an Attention item unanswered for its SLA escalates (e.g. to Octo/Wilbur) rather than stalling silently (review MED)

constraints: Routing is declarative data (table), not hardcoded — advisors register subscriptions
Built EARLY (phase 1), not late — it is a prerequisite for advisor autonomy, not a capstone
The dispatcher is NOT exempt from observability or verification — it emits dispatch-audit events and is gate-certified
Cost circuit-breakers are HARD (pause at 100% budget) — autonomy never overrides a breaker
Idempotency + debounce mandatory — one mutation never thrashes N analyses
Hub-down never halts local rolling; hub-dependent work queues, spoke self-certifies with a marker
Every breaker trip / liveness failure / SLA breach surfaces on Attention with a reason
tests: Routing: emit a lug_mutation → only subscribed advisors (Expediter) wake; an unsubscribed advisor does not
Debounce: emit 20 lug_mutations in the debounce window → exactly ONE Expediter wake
Idempotency: re-emit an event whose wake is in-flight → no second wake
Rate breaker: an advisor exceeding its wakes/hour cap → paused + Attention alert with the rate
Burn breaker: drive projected spend to 80% → alert; to 100% → autonomous dispatch PAUSES + Attention
Loop guard: an advisor that fails and re-emits its trigger K times → circuit-broken + escalated
Liveness: kill a running advisor → heartbeat silence >5m → its work requeued + Attention raised
Dispatcher self-gate: every dispatch produces a dispatch-audit event; a Pattern Gate certifies dispatcher health
Hub-down: make the hub unreachable → local dispatch/Autopilot/gates continue; hub-dependent work flagged hub_pending; spoke self-certifies with hub-unreachable marker
Attention SLA: an unanswered escalation past SLA escalates to Octo, does not stall silently

health.spec_drift_risk: low — initial spec authored from blind-review finding
open_questions: Dispatcher process model: long-running daemon vs cron-tick + event-journal drain vs git-hook triggers (proposed: event-journal drain on a short cadence tick — survives crashes, no daemon to babysit)
Budget thresholds: per-spoke fixed vs Navigator/Assessor-informed dynamic (proposed: Navigator-informed, with a hard per-spoke ceiling)
Heartbeat mechanism for subagent-style advisors that are not long-lived processes

perceive: Read this spec + spec-observability-oversight-v1 (event bus it routes) + the S46 autonomy audit (current loose coupling) + ozi_autopilot.py + spoke_expediter.py (current manual invocation it replaces). Read epic AC15 (advisor analysis_trigger it honors).
execute: 1. Define the declarative routing table + advisor subscription schema. 2. Implement event-journal drain (crash-safe) matching events→subscribers with debounce + idempotency + analysis_trigger respect. 3. Implement the cost circuit-breaker (rate cap, burn cap with 80%/100% thresholds, loop guard). 4. Implement advisor liveness (heartbeat + requeue). 5. Emit dispatch-audit events; add the dispatcher's own Pattern Gate. 6. Implement hub-down local-continue + self-certify marker. 7. Wire the Attention SLA escalation.
verify: Run all tests[]. Confirm routing, debounce, idempotency, all three breakers (rate/burn/loop), liveness requeue, dispatcher self-gate, hub-down continuity, and Attention SLA escalation.
